info.title: Secure  AI Portal API Documentation
info.description: API documentation for the Secure AI Portal backend

put.description: Updates an existing agent form by re-copying the current agent state. Requires authentication and ownership.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the agent form

403.description: Forbidden (user does not own this form)

delete.description: Permanently deletes an agent form. Requires authentication and ownership.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the agent form

403.description: Forbidden (user does not own this form)

get.summary: Get public agent form for execution
get.description: Retrieves an agent form for public execution. Requires the secret UUID in query parameters.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the agent form

parameters.name: secret
parameters.description: Secret UUID for accessing the form

post.summary: Execute an agent form with streaming response
post.description: Executes an agent using the provided form variables. Returns a streaming SSE response with progress updates and AI output. Supports both single agents and chained agent execution.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the agent form

parameters.name: secret
parameters.description: Secret UUID for accessing the form

parameters.name: stream
parameters.description: Whether to stream the response (SSE) or return JSON

text/event-stream.description: SSE stream with progress and response data

post.summary: Publish an agent as a web form
post.description: Creates a web form for executing an agent by copying the agent's current state. Requires authentication and agent ownership.

agentId.description: The ID of the agent to publish

expiresAt.description: Optional expiration date for the form

url.description: Public URL for accessing the form

403.description: Forbidden (user does not own the agent)

get.description: Retrieves all agent forms created by the authenticated user.

post.summary: Create or get existing API key for an agent
post.description: Creates a dedicated API key for agent executions. If the agent already has an API key, returns the existing key ID.

existingApiKeyId.description: If the agent already has an API key, provide it to skip creation

200.description: API key created or retrieved successfully

delete.summary: Delete API key for an agent
delete.description: Deletes the dedicated API key for an agent

post.summary: Execute an agent via Web API
post.description: Executes a specific agent identified by UUID with provided variables. Requires Bearer token authentication if configured on the agent. Returns the agent's response directly in the API response.

parameters.name: uuid
parameters.description: The UUID of the agent to execute

schema.description: Object containing variables expected by the agent

executionTime.description: Time taken to execute in milliseconds

get.summary: NextAuth.js authentication endpoint (e.g., sign in, sign out, session).
get.description: Handles various authentication actions provided by NextAuth.js, based on the specific path segment (e.g., `/api/auth/signin`, `/api/auth/signout`, `/api/auth/session`, `/api/auth/providers`). Uses Keycloak as the identity provider if authentication is enabled. Bypasses standard authentication for secure share routes (`/secure-share/`, `/api/secure-share/`) by returning a dummy session.

parameters.name: nextauth
parameters.description: Path segments indicating the NextAuth action (e.g., `["signin"]`, `["callback", "keycloak"]`, `["session"]`). See NextAuth.js documentation for standard routes.

200.description: Successful authentication action (e.g., redirects for signin/signout, session data for `/session`). For secure share routes, returns a dummy session object.

302.description: Redirect response, typically for signin/signout flows.

401.description: Unauthorized, if authentication is required and fails.

post.summary: NextAuth.js authentication endpoint (e.g., sign in, sign out callbacks).
post.description: Handles POST requests for authentication actions, typically callbacks or signout. Uses Keycloak as the identity provider if authentication is enabled. Bypasses standard authentication for secure share routes (`/secure-share/`, `/api/secure-share/`) by returning a dummy session (though POST is less common for these routes).

parameters.name: nextauth
parameters.description: Path segments indicating the NextAuth action (e.g., `["signin", "keycloak"]`, `["signout"]`). See NextAuth.js documentation for standard routes.

requestBody.description: Request body varies depending on the NextAuth action (e.g., credentials for signin, CSRF token for signout).

200.description: Successful authentication action (e.g., session data after callback). For secure share routes, returns a dummy session object.

302.description: Redirect response, typically after callbacks or signout.

401.description: Unauthorized, if authentication is required and fails.

get.description:
Receives OAuth callback from enterprise Keycloak instance,
exchanges authorization code for tokens, and redirects to mobile app.

302.description: Redirect to mobile app with tokens or error

post.summary: Initiate enterprise OAuth flow with identity provider
post.description:
Initiates browser-based OAuth authentication for enterprise instances
that use federated identity providers (e.g., Microsoft, Google, SAML).
Returns authorization URL for mobile app to open in system browser.

authorization_url.description: URL to open for OAuth authentication

post.description:
Triggers a password reset email for the specified email address.
Only available when accounting type is 'consumer'.
Returns success even if email doesn't exist to prevent user enumeration.

200.description: Password reset email sent (or email doesn't exist - always returns success for security)

403.description: Password reset not available (not in consumer mode)

post.summary: Authenticate using Keycloak Direct Access Grants (consumer mode)
post.description:
Authenticates user via Keycloak's Resource Owner Password Credentials flow.
This endpoint is used by the custom login UI in consumer mode.
Returns tokens that are used to create a NextAuth session via the credentials provider.

200.description: Authentication successful - returns tokens for session creation

get.description: Constructs the Keycloak OpenID Connect logout URL using environment variables (`KEYCLOAK_ISSUER`, `KEYCLOAK_ID`, `NEXTAUTH_URL` or request headers) and redirects the user to it. This effectively logs the user out of both the application and the Keycloak session. Does not require the user to be authenticated with the application beforehand, as Keycloak handles its own session.

307.description: Redirects the user to the Keycloak logout endpoint.

Location.description: The URL of the Keycloak logout endpoint with client_id and redirect_uri parameters.

405.description: Method not allowed (only GET is supported).

500.description: Internal server error (e.g., Keycloak configuration incomplete, could not determine origin).

post.summary: Refresh Keycloak access token (mobile app support)
post.description:
Proxies token refresh requests from mobile apps to Keycloak.
This endpoint is necessary because mobile apps cannot securely store
the client_secret required for confidential OAuth clients.

The backend adds the client_secret and forwards the request to Keycloak,
then returns the new tokens to the mobile app.

refresh_token.description: The refresh token to use for obtaining new tokens

200.description: Token refresh successful - returns new tokens

post.description:
Securely refreshes an Office 365 access token using a refresh token.
This endpoint handles the client secret on the backend to ensure security.
Requires user authentication.

refresh_token.description: Refresh token from previous token exchange

access_token.description: New access token for Microsoft Graph API

refresh_token.description: New refresh token (may be the same as input)

400.description: Bad request - missing or invalid refresh token

500.description: Internal server error - token refresh failed

post.summary: Exchange Office 365 authorization code for access tokens
post.description:
Securely exchanges an Office 365 authorization code for access and refresh tokens.
This endpoint handles the client secret on the backend to ensure security.
Requires user authentication.

code.description: Authorization code from Office 365 OAuth callback

redirect_uri.description: Redirect URI that was used in the authorization request

access_token.description: Access token for Microsoft Graph API

400.description: Bad request - missing or invalid parameters

403.description: Forbidden - Office 365 integration not enabled or domain not allowed

500.description: Internal server error - token exchange failed

post.description:
Creates a new user account with Keycloak and sets up initial credits.
Only available when accounting type is 'consumer'.
Payment method defaults to pay-as-you-go if not specified.
Users can set up payment details after signup.

email.description: User's email address (will be used as username)

400.description: Bad request - invalid input or validation error

403.description: Registration not available (not in consumer mode)

post.description:
Triggers Keycloak to resend the email verification link to a user who hasn't verified their email yet.
Proxies request to account-handler which has the necessary Keycloak admin permissions.

email.description: User's email address to resend verification to

429.description: Too many requests - rate limit exceeded

post.description:
Confirms password reset with token and sets new password.
Only available when accounting type is 'consumer'.

400.description: Bad request - invalid token or password

403.description: Password reset not available (not in consumer mode)

404.description: Reset token not found or expired

post.description:
Verifies a user's email address using a verification token from the email link.
This is a server-side proxy to the account-handler service to avoid exposing internal URLs.

get.summary: Serve static assets from memory cache
get.description:
Serves static images (PNG, JPG, SVG, ICO, GIF, WEBP) from an in-memory cache
for optimal performance. Falls back to filesystem if asset is not cached.
Assets are preloaded into memory at server startup.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: Path to the static asset (e.g., icons/openai.svg, images/wizard/multi-model.png)

Cache-Control.description: Aggressive caching header for immutable assets

get.summary: Retrieves the content of the project changelog.
get.description: Reads the `CHANGELOG` file from the project root directory and returns its content. Requires authentication if enabled.

content.description: The content of the CHANGELOG file.

405.description: Method not allowed (only GET is supported).

500.description: Internal server error reading the changelog file.

get.summary: Fetch a chat attachment by ID
get.description: Returns metadata by default, base64 JSON when format=base64, or downloadable raw binary when format=raw.

get.summary: Fetch a chat attachment thumbnail by ID
get.description: Returns raw thumbnail image binary for a stored chat attachment.

post.summary: Handles chat requests, interacts with LLMs, and streams responses.
post.description: This endpoint processes chat messages, interacts with configured Large Language Models (LLMs) via a proxy or directly, supports system prompts, temperature settings, plugins (including reasoning boost), and merges plugin @mentions from the last user message into the enabled plugin list before augmentation. It also supports file content injection, and streams the LLM's response back to the client. It handles token counting, context window management, and error handling for LLM interactions. Uses Edge Runtime for streaming capabilities.

200.description: Successful response, streaming the LLM's output. The response is a text stream (text/event-stream or similar, depending on the underlying `OpenAIStream` implementation).

schema.description: Streamed text response from the LLM.

400.description: Bad request (e.g., invalid JSON payload, missing required fields).

500.description: Internal server error (e.g., error initializing Tiktoken, LLM API error, proxy error).

504.description: Gateway timeout (e.g., LLM request timed out).

get.description: Returns the current queue status for a conversation including queued items with previews

delete.description: Removes a message from the conversation queue at the specified index

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: Index of the queue item to remove (0-based)

post.description: Moves a queue item from one position to another

fromIndex.description: Current index of the item to move

toIndex.description: Target index to move the item to

get.description: Returns public configuration values needed by the client-side application

portalHeadline.description: Optional override for the main headline on the initial chat view

portalSubline.description: Optional override for the subtitle on the initial chat view

accountingType.description: Type of accounting - consumer shows balance, enterprise does not

accountingAllowSelfDeletion.description: Whether users can delete their own accounts

oauthEnabled.description: Whether OAuth social login is enabled (consumer mode only)

defaultCreditPackages.description: Default credit packages available for purchase

credits.description: Number of credits in the package

releaseChannel.description: Release channel for the deployment (dev, prod, stable)

isOffice365Auth.description: Whether the authentication system is configured for Office 365

office365Enabled.description: Whether Office 365 mailbox integration is enabled

office365ClientId.description: Azure AD Application Client ID for Office 365 integration

office365TenantId.description: Azure AD Tenant ID (common for multi-tenant)

office365AllowedDomains.description: List of allowed email domains for Office 365 integration

office365Scopes.description: Microsoft Graph API scopes for Office 365 integration

teamMembersEnabled.description: Whether Team Members feature is enabled

teamMemberPromptInspectorEnabled.description: Whether the Team Member prompt inspector is enabled

hasAccess.description: Whether current user has access to this extension

post.summary: Summarize selected conversation messages into a single condensed paragraph.
post.description: Takes a list of conversation messages and returns an AI-generated summary that preserves key facts and context. Used by the Conversation Compactor feature.

model.description: Model ID to use for summarization. Defaults to DEFAULT_MODEL.

maxTokens.description: Target summary length in tokens. Defaults to 500.

get.summary: Retrieves documentation content or lists available documents.
get.description: Fetches Markdown documentation files stored server-side within the `public/docs` directory. Can operate in two modes based on query parameters: 1. `list=true`: Returns a structure listing available languages and the documents within each. 2. `lang=<language>&file=<filename>`: Returns the content of a specific Markdown file, processing image paths. Authentication is currently optional but can be enabled.

parameters.name: list
parameters.description: If true, returns the list of available documents instead of content.

parameters.name: lang
parameters.description: The language directory of the document to fetch (e.g., 'en'). Required if `list` is not true.

parameters.name: file
parameters.description: The filename of the document to fetch (e.g., 'introduction.md'). Required if `list` is not true.

200.description: Successfully retrieved documentation list or content.

title.description: The title extracted from the Markdown file.

content.description: The Markdown content with processed image paths.

400.description: Bad request (e.g., missing parameters, invalid path).

404.description: Not found (e.g., documentation directory or specific file not found).

405.description: Method not allowed (only GET is supported).

get.description: Securely proxies extension sub-resources (CSS, JS, images, etc.) from backend
Returns a single external source entry from cache or connector.

put.description: Updates sync settings for an external source credential.

delete.description: Deletes the external source credential for the authenticated user.

post.description: Forces a refresh of the external source index.

get.description: Returns cached tree entries for the specified external source.

post.summary: Connect a new external knowledge source
post.description: Creates a new external source credential stored in the accounting backend.

get.description: Returns external source connections for the authenticated user (sanitized).

post.description: Executes a safe, server-side external source action without exposing credentials.

get.description: Searches cached external source entries for the authenticated user.

parameters.name: sourceIds
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of source IDs to search

post.summary: Generates a creative title for a media item (image/music/video).
post.description: Takes the generation prompt and media type, sends them to an LLM (attempting Qwen 3 Coder first, then falling back to GPT-5 Mini) to generate an artistic, creative title for the media. Uses Edge Runtime.

mediaType.description: The type of media being titled.

prompt.description: The generation prompt used to create the media.

provider.description: The provider/model used to generate the media.

previousTitles.description: Optional array of recent titles to avoid repetition.

apiKey.description: Optional API key to use for title generation.

post.summary: Generates a concise title for a conversation.
post.description: Takes the first user message and optionally the assistant's response, sends them to an LLM (attempting a primary model first, then falling back to a secondary) Primary model: Qwen 3 Coder, Fallback: GPT-5 Mini to generate a short, descriptive title for the conversation. If assistantResponse is missing, generates title from user message only. Returns the generated title or a default title if generation fails. Uses Edge Runtime.

userMessage.description: The content of the first user message in the conversation.

assistantResponse.description: The content of the first assistant response in the conversation (optional - if missing, title generated from user message only).

model.description: The model used for the conversation (used for context, not necessarily for title generation).

encryption.description: Whether the connection to the LLM proxy requires encryption.

apiKey.description: Optional API key to use for title generation (e.g., user's primary API key).

previousTitles.description: Optional array of recent conversation titles from the same folder for context (up to 5).

500.description: Internal server error (e.g., LLM API error during title generation, handler error).

get.description: Returns list of available research sources from the grounding service
Returns a simple status message to indicate the API is running.
Returns a simple status message ('ok') to indicate the API is running. This endpoint does not require authentication.

get.summary: Get status of a specific image generation run
get.description: Returns the current status and details of a run from in-memory state, with fallback to persisted storage. Use for polling.

delete.description: Cancels a pending/processing run or deletes a completed/failed run

get.summary: List image generation runs for the authenticated user
get.description: Returns all active and recent completed runs for the user

post.summary: Create a new image generation run
post.description: Starts an async image generation task. Returns immediately with run_id for polling.

contextReferences.description: Optional chat/file references for prompt enhancement

get.summary: Fetch a generated image by ID
get.description: Returns base64 JSON by default or downloadable raw binary when format=raw.

parameters.name: size
parameters.description: thumbnail returns a small JPEG (~350px max) for avatar display. Falls back to full image if thumbnail unavailable.

delete.summary: Delete a generated image by ID

post.description: Internal endpoint invoked by a scheduled job. Fetches per-user storage usage from chatstorage and forwards usage to account-handler for billing. Requires a shared secret in the X-Webhook-Secret or Authorization header.

parameters.name: X-Webhook-Secret
parameters.description: Shared secret for internal billing authentication.

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description: Bearer token containing the shared secret.

get.description: Returns Knowledgebase asset metadata for the authenticated user.

parameters.name: includeData
parameters.description: Include fileData, markdownContent, extractedText, and thumbnailData in the response.

get.description: Streams the asset file for an authorized Knowledgebase entry.
Streams the thumbnail for a Knowledgebase asset when available.

post.description: Stores a Knowledgebase asset and optional extracted/markdown content.

get.description: Returns a single knowledgebase entry by ID.

patch.description: Updates an existing knowledgebase entry by ID.

delete.description: Deletes a knowledgebase entry by ID.

get.description: Returns version snapshots for a knowledgebase entry.

parameters.name: includeEntryData
parameters.description: Include full entry snapshots in the response.

get.description: Returns all knowledgebase entries for the authenticated user.

parameters.name: includeShared
parameters.description: Include entries shared with the authenticated user.

parameters.name: sharedOnly
parameters.description: Only return entries shared with the authenticated user.

post.description: Creates a new knowledgebase entry for the authenticated user.

patch.description: Updates scope, permission, and recipients for an existing Knowledgebase share.

delete.description: Removes a Knowledgebase share owned by the authenticated user.

get.description: Returns Knowledgebase shares owned by the authenticated user.

post.description: Creates a new Knowledgebase share entry for the authenticated user.

get.description: Returns a single knowledgebase entry version snapshot.

post.description: Restores a knowledgebase entry to the specified version snapshot.

get.summary: Return all media runs for the authenticated user
get.description: Aggregates image, music, and video runs into a single response for polling fallback.

post.summary: Fetch and extract readable text from a web page URL
post.description: Accepts an absolute URL (`https://example.com`) or a bare domain (`example.com`, `www.example.com`). Bare domains are automatically normalized to `https://` before fetching. Performs SSRF validation, fetches the remote page, and returns Readability-extracted plain text suitable for use as a Media Studio context reference.

415.description: Remote page content type is not supported

422.description: Readable text could not be extracted from the page

502.description: Remote server returned a non-OK status

504.description: Remote page took too long to load

post.summary: Summarize oversized media reference text for context fitting.
post.description: Compresses extracted file text into a smaller summary that can fit model context constraints in Media Studio.

text.description: Extracted text from uploaded reference file.

maxTokens.description: Maximum token budget for the returned summary.

context.description: Optional context label used to guide summarization focus.

post.description: Starts image, music, or video generation runs for internal tools.

post.summary: Upload user images to Media Studio library
post.description: Uploads a user-provided image, validates and normalizes it with server-side processing, generates a thumbnail, and stores it in the image library.

get.description: Returns a single memory entry by ID.

patch.description: Updates an existing memory entry by ID.

delete.description: Deletes a memory entry by ID.

get.description: Returns memory entries for the authenticated user.

post.description: Creates a new memory entry for the authenticated user.

get.description: Returns personal memory entries for the specified Team Members.

schema.description: Comma-separated list of Team Member IDs

post.description: Executes allowed internal memory tools for the authenticated user.

Second and final step of the 2-stage mobile account deletion process. This endpoint performs the actual deletion of all user data and disables the user account.
**Security Features:** - Requires valid Bearer token authentication - Requires valid verification token from initiation step - Validates device consistency between initiation and execution - Requires final confirmation phrase - Performs complete data deletion with comprehensive logging
**Final Validation Requirements:** - Verification token from initiation step must be valid and unexpired - Final confirmation phrase must be exactly "PERMANENTLY DELETE MY ACCOUNT" - Device information must match initiation request - User must provide current timestamp for replay protection
**Data Deletion Process:** 1. Delete all conversations and dialog history 2. Delete all custom agents and configurations 3. Delete all shared content and links 4. Delete all API keys and access tokens 5. Delete user settings and preferences 6. Disable user account in Keycloak (safer than deletion) 7. Clean up verification tokens
**WARNING: This action is irreversible and immediate.**

deletionId.description: Unique ID for this deletion operation (audit trail)

deletedData.description: Summary of what was actually deleted

durationMs.description: How long the deletion took in milliseconds

sessionTokensDestroyed.description: Whether all session tokens were cleaned up

message.example: Verification token not found or invalid

401.description: Unauthorized - invalid or missing Bearer token

post.description:
First step of the 2-stage mobile account deletion process. This endpoint initiates the deletion workflow, counts user data, and returns a verification token that must  be used in the execution step.
**Mobile-Specific Security Features:** - Requires valid Bearer token authentication - Rate limiting: Maximum 2 deletion attempts per 24 hours per user - Device fingerprinting for additional security - Enhanced confirmation requirements - Real-time data counting and warnings - Generates time-limited verification token (expires in 30 minutes)
**2-Stage Mobile Process:** 1. **Initiate** (this endpoint) - Verify intent, count data, generate token 2. **Execute** - Final deletion with verification token
This 2-endpoint design prevents accidental deletion and provides users with clear information about what will be deleted before final execution.

confirmationPhrase.example: I WANT TO DELETE MY ACCOUNT

timestamp.description: Unix timestamp when request was initiated

acknowledgeDataLoss.description: Confirmation that user understands all data will be lost

200.description: Initiation completed successfully, ready for execution

verificationToken.description: Verification token for execution step (expires in 30 minutes)

dataToDelete.description: Exact counts of data that will be deleted

example: You have 47 conversations that will be permanently deleted
You have 12 custom agents that will be permanently deleted
You have 5 shared items that will become inaccessible

finalConfirmationRequired.description: The exact phrase required for final execution

message.example: The confirmation phrase must exactly match the required text

401.description: Unauthorized - invalid or missing Bearer token

403.description: Forbidden - account deletion not enabled or rate limited

post.description: Starts an async image generation task that can be monitored via status endpoint

negative_prompt.description: Negative prompt to avoid certain elements

fast_mode.description: Enable Lightning Mode for faster generation

get.description: Returns all generated images for a completed task

parameters.name: taskId
parameters.description: Task ID returned from generate endpoint

403.description: Access denied - task belongs to different user

404.description: Task not found or no images available

get.description: Returns the current status and progress of an image generation task

parameters.name: taskId
parameters.description: Task ID returned from generate endpoint

403.description: Access denied - task belongs to different user

get.summary: List image generation tasks for user
get.description: Returns a list of recent image generation tasks for the authenticated user

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of tasks to return

parameters.name: since
parameters.description: Return tasks created since this timestamp (ISO 8601)

total.description: Total number of tasks for user (before limit)

has_more.description: Whether there are more tasks beyond the limit

get.summary: Retrieves the list of available AI models.
get.description: Returns an array of configured and enabled AI models, including their properties like ID, name, token limits, capabilities, and default settings. Always returns at least one default model, even in case of errors. Authentication may be checked for specific methods (e.g., POST) if enabled.

200.description: A list of available AI models.

401.description: Unauthorized (only applicable if auth is required for GET, currently seems POST-specific).

500.description: Internal server error (returns default model list).

post.summary: Retrieves the list of available AI models (potentially with authentication check).
post.description: Identical to the GET request in terms of returned data. However, if authentication is enabled (`NEXT_PUBLIC_AUTH_ENABLED=true`), this endpoint WILL perform an authentication check and return 401 if the user is not logged in. Used by the frontend to fetch models potentially after a user action that requires auth.

200.description: A list of available AI models.

500.description: Internal server error (returns default model list).

get.description: Returns the current polling state for a multi-dialog conversation.

post.description: Starts a new music generation run in proxy or direct mode.
Mobile-friendly endpoint that creates a music run and starts background generation.

400.description: Missing required fields or invalid input.

post.summary: Generate lyrics for a music prompt
post.description: Generates lyrics via LLM proxy in production, or calls the music service directly when MEDIA_SERVICES_DIRECT_MODE is enabled.

prompt.description: The lyrics prompt to generate from.

get.description: Returns folders/files for OneDrive root or for the requested folder ID using the user's connected Office 365 credential.

parameters.name: folderId
parameters.description: Optional OneDrive folder ID. Omit to browse root.

post.description: Downloads a OneDrive file, sends it through file-handler, and returns extracted FileContent compatible with chat attachments.

fileId.description: OneDrive item ID for the file to process

maxContextTokens.description: Optional max token budget for extracted content

applyTruncation.description: Truncate extracted text to fit maxContextTokens when supported

404.description: Office 365 connection not found or file missing

get.description: Searches the authenticated user's OneDrive using Microsoft Graph and returns file/folder results.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: Search term. Alias `q` is also supported.

get.description: Validates Office 365 credential access and checks whether the user's OneDrive is reachable.

post.summary: Detect relevant plugins for a user message
post.description: Runs a fast backend preflight check (regex/keyword rules) to detect which plugins should be auto-enabled for the next send.

get.summary: Get available plugins for the authenticated user
get.description: Returns a list of plugins available to the user. If dynamic plugins are enabled, it will fetch user-specific plugins using their primary API key. Supports both session-based (web) and Bearer token (mobile) authentication.

description.example: Search the internet for real-time information

systemPrompt.example:
tool_name: websearch
search_term: This is an example search

401.description: Unauthorized - Authentication required (session or Bearer token)

post.summary: Refresh plugins from the RAG index
post.description: Triggers a refresh of the plugin list from the dynamic RAG index. Requires admin privileges. Supports both session-based (web) and Bearer token (mobile) authentication.

401.description: Unauthorized - Authentication required (session or Bearer token)

get.summary: Get media for a shared collection item
get.description:
Returns full media payload (audio/video/image) for a specific item in a shared playlist/gallery.
Access is controlled by share secret (and optional password).

parameters.name: mediaId
parameters.description: Media ID belonging to the shared collection.

400.description: Invalid parameters or unsupported collection/media type.

404.description: Share or media item not found.

get.summary: Get active AI radio station for current user

post.summary: Create and start a new AI radio station
Get AI model recommendations based on user context
post.description:
Analyzes the user's input context (message, attachments, plugins) and
returns ranked model recommendations. Uses rule-based analysis with
model quality attributes from the backend.

File attachments are analyzed via metadata (file type, token count),
NOT by reading the actual file content. This allows efficient
recommendations without re-processing large files.

attachmentTokens.description: Total token count of all attached files

hasCodeAttachments.description: Whether any code files are attached

hasLargeAttachments.description: Whether any attachment exceeds typical context limits (>50k tokens)

maxResults.description: Maximum number of recommendations to return

currentModelId.description: Current model ID to exclude from recommendations (avoids suggesting the model already in use)

contextHash.description: Hash of the analyzed context for caching

usedLLM.description: Whether LLM was used for analysis (always false currently)

503.description: Service unavailable - model data not available

post.summary: Search dialogs by title or content
post.description:
Searches user's dialogs by title or message content.
This endpoint proxies to the chatstorage backend which performs
the actual database search. Requires authentication.

Security features:
- Session authentication required
- Frontend input validation (defense in depth)
- Rate limiting enforced by backend (10 requests/minute)
- User can only search their own dialogs

folderId.description: Folder UUID if in a folder

400.description: Invalid query (too short, too long, or invalid characters)

get.summary: Retrieves a specific conversation from server-side storage.
get.description: Fetches a conversation object based on the provided `conversationId`. Requires authentication if enabled. Uses a helper function (`loadConversationFromStorage`) to interact with the storage backend.

parameters.name: conversationId
parameters.description: The ID of the conversation to retrieve.

404.description: Conversation not found (handled by `loadConversationFromStorage`, may result in 500 if error not specific).

405.description: Method not allowed (only GET is supported).

500.description: Internal server error (e.g., failed to load from storage).

get.description: Fetches the settings object for the authenticated user from the storage service. Requires authentication.

200.description: Successfully retrieved settings. Returns an empty object if no settings found.

400.description: Bad request (e.g., User ID missing from session).

500.description: Internal server error or error fetching from storage.

post.description: Saves the provided settings object for the authenticated user to the storage service. Requires authentication.

schema.description: The settings object to save in YAML format.

200.description: Successfully saved settings. Returns the saved settings object.

400.description: Bad request (e.g., User ID missing from session).

500.description: Internal server error or error saving to storage.

get.description: Fetches a specific shared conversation record using the provided share `id`. If the share is private, authentication is required. If a `secret` query parameter is provided (for secure shares), it validates it against the stored secret UUID. Updates the access count for the share. Returns the conversation messages and metadata about the share.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the shared conversation record.

parameters.name: secret
parameters.description: The secret UUID for accessing secure shares (public or private).

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description: Bearer token for mobile app authentication (optional, falls back to session cookies).

401.description: Unauthorized (required for private shares if not logged in).

403.description: Forbidden (invalid secret UUID provided for a secure share).

405.description: Method not allowed (only GET and DELETE are supported).

delete.description: Deletes a specific shared conversation record. Requires authentication. The authenticated user must be the owner of the share to delete it. Supports both session-based authentication (cookies) and Bearer token authentication.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the shared conversation record to delete.

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description: Bearer token for mobile app authentication (optional, falls back to session cookies).

403.description: Forbidden (user does not own this share link).

post.summary: Creates a share link for a conversation.
post.description: Creates a share record for a specified conversation. Requires authentication via session cookies or Bearer token. The user must own the conversation. Loads the conversation content from storage, verifies ownership, strips internal tool result messages and `<internal_tool>` tags, then calls a utility function (`createShareRecord`) to persist the share information (including sanitized conversation content) in the storage backend. Returns the ID and secret UUID of the newly created share record.

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description: Bearer token for mobile app authentication (optional, falls back to session cookies).

conversationId.description: The ID of the conversation to share.

shareType.description: The type of share (public or private).

expiresAt.description: Optional expiration date for the share link.

password.description: Optional password for encrypting the shared content.

id.description: The ID of the created share record.

secretUuid.description: The secret UUID required to access the share (used in the share link).

400.description: Bad request (e.g., missing conversationId, invalid ID format, invalid shareType).

401.description: Unauthorized (user not signed in, no valid session or Bearer token).

403.description: Forbidden (user does not own the conversation).

404.description: Conversation not found or verification failed.

405.description: Method not allowed (only POST is supported).

500.description: Internal server error (e.g., failed to load conversation, failed to create share record, storage service error).

Builds a single MP3 track for an entire conversation and supports role-based voices.
Converts one text payload to MP3 audio for inline playback and download.

Takes a text payload, rewrites it via an LLM into a two-host podcast dialogue,
stores the resulting segments server-side, and returns a `stream_url` that the
client can assign to an HTMLAudioElement to begin progressive multi-voice MP3 playback.

get.description:
Iterates over each dialogue segment from the session, generating TTS with the
appropriate host voice and writing each MP3 chunk immediately for progressive playback.

post.description:
Validates TTS parameters and stores them server-side as a short-lived session.
Returns a `stream_url` that the client can assign to an `HTMLAudioElement.src` to begin
progressive MP3 playback immediately as audio data is generated.

stream_url.description: URL to GET for progressive MP3 audio streaming.

get.summary: Stream MP3 audio for a TTS session
get.description: Streams progressive MP3 audio, writing each generated chunk immediately so playback can begin before all chunks are complete.

post.summary: Upload and process any file type with SSE streaming progress.
post.description: Canonical web-portal upload route. Accepts all file types and wraps every response in an SSE envelope. Images and long-running documents (PPTX, DOCX, PDF) stream real-time progress events. Office files ≤ 50 MB are persisted as downloadable chat attachments in chatstorage. Rate limited to 20 uploads per minute per IP, matching /api/upload.

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description: Bearer token for mobile app authentication (optional, falls back to session cookies).

filename.description: The original name of the file.

processing_mode.description: How images are processed before sending to the model provider.

store_user_image.description: When true, store image blobs for chat thumbnails (images only).

conversation_id.description: Optional conversation ID for associating blobs and attachments.

message_node_id.description: Optional message node ID for associating blobs and attachments.

max_context_tokens.description: Optional context token limit; triggers exceeds_context_limit in result when exceeded.

apply_truncation.description: When true, apply truncation to fit context limits.

200.description: SSE stream of upload/processing events. The final `phase: complete` event result includes image_id/thumbnail_url for images and file_id/thumbnail_url for persisted office attachments.

400.description: Bad request (missing file, unsupported content type).

429.description: Rate limit exceeded (20 uploads per minute per IP).

500.description: Internal server error or error during file processing.

post.description: Accepts a file (content and filename), processes it using an external file handler service, and returns extracted text and metadata. Requires authentication via session cookies or Bearer token. The file-handler service is authenticated using the LLM API key. Supported office files up to 50MB (doc/docx, ppt/pptx, xls/xlsx, pdf) are additionally persisted as raw chat attachments in chatstorage.

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description: Bearer token for mobile app authentication (optional, falls back to session cookies).

filename.description: The original name of the file.

include_markdown.description: When true, return markdown conversion if available.

processing_mode.description: How images are processed before sending to the model provider.

store_user_image.description: When true, store image blobs for chat thumbnails.

conversation_id.description: Optional conversation ID for associating user image blobs.

message_node_id.description: Optional message node ID for associating user image blobs.

max_context_tokens.description: Optional context token limit for the file.

apply_truncation.description: When true, apply truncation to fit context limits.

quick_analysis.description: When true, use quick analysis mode when supported.

formId.description: Optional form ID to use a form-specific API key.

formSecret.description: Optional form secret to use a form-specific API key.

text.description: Extracted text content from the file.

filesize.description: Size of the file in bytes.

image_id.description: Stored image ID for user-uploaded images.

file_id.description: Stored chat attachment ID for office files.

thumbnail.description: Base64-encoded office thumbnail preview from file-handler.

page_renders.description: Base64-encoded preview pages/slides for office files.

thumbnail_url.description: Thumbnail URL for stored user-uploaded images or office chat attachments.

400.description: Bad request (e.g., missing content, filename, unsupported file type).

401.description: Unauthorized (authentication required, invalid session or Bearer token).

405.description: Method not allowed (only POST is supported).

500.description: Internal server error or error during file processing.

504.description: Gateway timeout (file handler service timed out).

get.description: Returns base64 JSON by default or downloadable raw binary when format=raw.

get.summary: Fetch a user-uploaded image thumbnail by ID

Checks if the current user's account has been marked as deleted. This endpoint provides a way to verify account status without requiring broad Keycloak permissions. If the account is deleted, returns 410 Gone.
⚠️ IMPORTANT: This is an EDGE CASE endpoint. Use sparingly: - Only call when user explicitly initiates account-related actions - DO NOT poll automatically or on every page load - Account deletion is a rare event that should not trigger frequent checks

410.description: Account has been deleted and is no longer accessible

message.example: This account has been permanently deleted and can no longer access the system.

post.description:
Permanently deletes the authenticated user's account and all associated data. This action is irreversible and includes: - Deletion of all conversations and dialog history - Deletion of all agents and agent forms - Deletion of all shares and shared content - Deletion of all API keys and account settings - Disabling the user account in Keycloak
**Security Requirements:** - User must be authenticated - Account self-deletion must be enabled in server configuration - Request must include confirmation token and user email verification - Protection against accidental deletion through multiple validation steps
**Warning:** This action cannot be undone. All data will be permanently lost.

userEmail.description: User's email address for verification (must match authenticated user)

understandConsequences.description: User confirmation that they understand this action is irreversible

currentTimestamp.description: Current timestamp in milliseconds (must be within 5 minutes of server time)

405.description: Method not allowed - only POST is supported

500.description: Internal server error during deletion process

post.description:
Final step of the multi-stage account deletion process. This endpoint performs the actual deletion of all user data and disables the user account.
**Security Features:** - Requires valid session token from initiation step - Requires valid verification token from verification step - Both tokens must be unexpired and belong to the same session - Validates user identity one final time - Requires final confirmation phrase - Performs complete data deletion with comprehensive logging
**Final Validation Requirements:** - Session token from initiation step must be valid - Verification token from verification step must be valid - Final confirmation phrase must be exactly "EXECUTE_DELETION_NOW" - User must provide current timestamp for replay protection
**Data Deletion Process:** 1. Delete all conversations and dialog history 2. Delete all custom agents and configurations 3. Delete all shared content and links 4. Delete all API keys and access tokens 5. Delete user settings and preferences 6. Disable user account in Keycloak (safer than deletion) 7. Clean up all session tokens
**WARNING: This action is irreversible and immediate.**

deletionId.description: Unique ID for this deletion operation (audit trail)

deletedData.description: Summary of what was actually deleted

sessionTokensDestroyed.description: Whether all session tokens were cleaned up

post.description:
First step of the multi-stage account deletion process. This endpoint initiates the deletion workflow and returns a session token that must be used in subsequent steps.
**Security Features:** - Requires valid user authentication - Account deletion must be enabled in configuration - Generates time-limited session token (expires in 30 minutes) - Rate limited to prevent abuse - Logs initiation for audit trail
**Multi-Stage Process:** 1. **Initiate** (this endpoint) - Generate deletion session 2. **Verify** - Email and security confirmations 3. **Execute** - Final deletion with all tokens
This multi-endpoint design prevents CSRF attacks and makes it much harder for malicious actors to trick users into account deletion.

acknowledgeIrreversible.description: Initial acknowledgment that deletion is irreversible

userAgent.description: User agent string for security logging

sessionToken.description: Session token for this deletion process (expires in 30 minutes)

nextStep.description: URL for the next step in the process

email.description: User's email (for verification in next step)

accountAge.description: How long the account has existed

estimatedDataCount.description: Rough estimate of data that will be deleted

post.summary: Verify account deletion details and generate verification token
post.description:
Second step of the multi-stage account deletion process. This endpoint verifies user identity, gathers data counts, and generates a verification token required for the final execution step.
**Security Features:** - Requires valid session token from initiation step - Validates user email confirmation - Checks exact confirmation phrase matching - Generates time-limited verification token (expires in 15 minutes) - Gathers actual data counts for user review - Logs verification for audit trail
**Verification Requirements:** - Session token from initiation step must be valid and not expired - User email must match exactly (case-sensitive) - Confirmation phrase must be exactly "DELETE_MY_ACCOUNT" - User must acknowledge understanding of data loss
This step provides the user with exact counts of what will be deleted and requires explicit confirmation before allowing final execution.

emailConfirmation.description: User's email address for verification (must match exactly)

acknowledgeDataLoss.description: Confirmation that user understands all data will be lost

verificationToken.description: Verification token for final execution step (expires in 15 minutes)

nextStep.description: URL for the final execution step

dataToDelete.description: Exact counts of data that will be deleted

example: You have 47 conversations that will be permanently deleted
You have 12 custom agents that will be permanently deleted
You have 5 shared items that will become inaccessible

get.description: Returns device usage aggregates for user, organization, or global scope.
Returns the primary API key for the authenticated user. Deployment fallbacks apply only when the user is signed in; unauthenticated requests do not receive proxy-global keys.

apiKey.description: The user's primary API key or fallback global key

get.description: Retrieve a filtered list of products for the authenticated user's organization. This endpoint is ideal for populating sales pages and product catalogs.

parameters.name: region
parameters.description: Region name for localized pricing (e.g., 'United States', 'Germany', 'Japan'). When provided, {{sparks}} placeholders in product display names will be replaced with region-specific amounts.

is_inherited.description: Whether product is inherited from parent organization

localizations.description: Localized content by full locale code (e.g., en_US, en_GB, es_ES)

sparksAmount.description: Locale-specific sparks amount (may vary by region)

503.description: Service unavailable - Account Handler not configured

get.description: Retrieve detailed information about a specific product. Returns 404 for non-live products to prevent information leakage.

404.description: Product not found or not live

503.description: Service unavailable - Account Handler not configured

get.description: Retrieve products formatted specifically for a particular platform. Returns platform-optimized data. Only live products are included.

parameters.name: region
parameters.description: Region name for localized pricing (e.g., 'United States', 'Germany', 'Japan'). When provided, {{sparks}} placeholders in product display names will be replaced with region-specific amounts.

parameters.name: language
parameters.description: Language preference in locale format (e.g., 'en_US', 'de_DE', 'es_ES'). Used for localized content selection.

apple_id.description: Apple product ID (when platform is apple)

google_id.description: Google product ID (when platform is google)

stripe_id.description: Stripe product ID (when platform is stripe)

localizations.description: Platform-specific localized content with full locale codes

503.description: Service unavailable - Account Handler not configured

delete.description: Revokes a specific Keycloak session belonging to the authenticated user.

get.description: Returns active Keycloak sessions for the authenticated user.

get.description: Returns the current subscription status from account-handler (replaces old Stripe-based endpoint)

get.summary: Retrieves usage data aggregated by API key
get.description: Fetches the user's token usage data grouped by API key from the Account Handler service. Requires authentication via session cookie (web) or Bearer token (mobile app).

500.description: Internal server error or Account Handler service unavailable

get.summary: Get usage statistics aggregated by AI model
get.description:
Returns detailed usage statistics broken down by AI model for the authenticated user.
Includes per-model metrics like requests, tokens, costs, and sparks spent.

parameters.name: start_date
parameters.description: Start date for usage period (ISO 8601 format)

parameters.name: end_date
parameters.description: End date for usage period (ISO 8601 format)

total_sparks.description: Total sparks spent across all models

get.summary: Get daily usage data broken down by model
get.description: Returns per-day, per-model usage for stacked area charts

get.summary: Retrieves usage data aggregated by day
get.description: Fetches the user's token usage data grouped by day from the Account Handler service. Returns daily breakdown of sparks spent and API calls for time-series visualization. Requires authentication via session cookie (web) or Bearer token (mobile app).

get.description: Returns the user's video runs without base64 payloads. In direct mode, in-progress runs are reconciled by checking the video service status.

post.description: Starts a new video generation run in proxy or direct mode.
Extends a video using the configured model. In production, routes through the LLM proxy; in direct mode, calls the video service directly.

model.description: Video model ID (must support extend capability).

video_id.description: Kie.ai task ID for the source video (from library metadata).

video_url.description: Deprecated alias for video_id (task ID only; URLs are not supported).

prompt.description: Extension prompt describing how to extend the video.

post.description: Mobile-friendly endpoint that creates a video run and starts background generation.

get.summary: List videos for the authenticated user
get.description: Returns video metadata and thumbnail URLs. Inline binary payloads are stripped unless include_video=true.

post.description: Proxies generation requests to the video service and storage requests to chatstorage.
Modifies a video using the configured model. In production, routes through the LLM proxy; in direct mode, calls the video service directly.

model.description: Video model ID (must support modify capability).

video_id.description: Video ID or temporary video URL to modify.

prompt.description: Modification prompt describing how to change the video.

post.summary: Upload a start image for video generation
post.description: Proxies a base64-encoded image upload to the video backend and returns a temporary download URL.

base64Data.description: Base64 data URI for the image.

post.summary: Upload a video for modify/extend workflows
post.description: Proxies a base64-encoded video upload to the video backend and returns a temporary download URL.

base64Data.description: Base64 data URI for the video.

post.description: Internal webhook to notify the portal that a user's balance has changed (purchases, vouchers, or external adjustments). Requires the shared BALANCE_WEBHOOK_SECRET in the X-Webhook-Secret or Authorization header.

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description: Bearer token containing the shared secret.

originalContent.description: Clean user message content before mention context injection.

model.description: Model ID used for this message (if different from conversation default)

contextOverride.description: If true, use provided messages without storage reload.

operator.description: The provider or operator of the model (e.g., OpenAI, Local, Google).

tokenLimit.description: Maximum number of tokens the model can handle.

prompt.description: Default system prompt for the model.

enabled.description: Whether the model is currently enabled for use.

modelType.description: Category or type of the model (e.g., Standard, Coding, Reasoning).

defaultTemperature.description: Default sampling temperature for the model.

iconFile.description: Optional icon filename associated with the model.

starred.description: Optional flag indicating if the user has starred this model.

activePath.description: Active branch path for branched conversations.

title.description: Title extracted from the Markdown file.

DocStructure.description: A map where keys are language codes (e.g., 'en') and values are arrays of DocFile objects for that language.

instance_id.description: Optional override for chatstorage instance ID.

instanceId.description: Optional override for chatstorage instance ID (camelCase).

error.description: Backend failure message, prefixed with "Generation failed on backend:"

audioStreamUrl.description: Stream URL for completed tracks - use instead of base64 audio data

error.description: Backend failure message, prefixed with "Generation failed on backend:"

videoStreamUrl.description: Stream URL for completed videos - use instead of base64 video data

url.description: Absolute URL (https://example.com) or bare domain (example.com / www.example.com). Bare domains are normalized to https://.
Final resolved URL after normalization and redirects

image_data.description: Base64 image data URL or raw base64 image payload

filename.description: Original file name from the client

mime_type.description: Original MIME type when image_data is raw base64

mode.description: Run mode; defaults to generate when omitted

prompt.description: Required for generate mode; optional for extend mode

lyrics.description: Full Control — exact lyrics (Kie prompt in custom mode); optional when instrumental

messages.description: Array of messages in the conversation.

model.description: The primary AI model used for the conversation.

prompt.description: System prompt used for the conversation.

temperature.description: Sampling temperature used for the conversation.

folderId.description: ID of the folder this conversation belongs to, or null.

owner.description: User ID of the conversation owner.

pseudonymization.description: Whether pseudonymization is enabled for this conversation.

encryption.description: Whether encryption is enabled for this conversation.

saved.description: Whether the conversation has been saved to persistent storage.

plugins.description: List of plugin IDs enabled for this conversation.

hasMoreMessages.description: Indicates if there are more messages available on the server (for pagination).

totalMessages.description: Total number of messages in the conversation on the server.

loadedMessages.description: Number of messages currently loaded in the `messages` array.

modelId.description: Optional model id to use for summarization.

encryption.description: Whether to use encrypted LLM proxy connection.

proxyConnection.description: Whether to use the LLM proxy connection.

title.description: Optional title. If omitted, one is auto-generated from the memory content.

payload.description: Raw tool payload (parsed JSON) or raw string

audio_data.description: Base64 data URL containing MP3 audio.

text.description: Text that should be converted to speech.
